lab.title: Lab 01 - Manage Microsoft Entra ID Identities

steps.title: Sign in to Azure Portal
steps.instruction: Sign in to the Azure portal at https://portal.azure.com. To proceed to the portal, select Cancel on the Welcome to Azure splash screen.
steps.aiResponse:
Good — I can see both screenshots now. The **before** shows the Azure loading splash, and the **after** shows the portal loaded to a **"Welcome to Azure!"** page — but it's NOT the dismissible splash/modal the instructions describe. It's the subscription landing page with no "Cancel" button.

The script's detection was wrong — the welcome screen **did** appear, but it's a different type than expected. The step is partially complete (signed in) but the dashboard isn't showing. Here's the accurate result:

```
ACTIONS_TAKEN: Navigated to https://portal.azure.com. Portal loaded with existing auth session (RudolfZhang@outlook account). No Cancel button was found on the welcome screen.
STEP_COMPLETED: YES (signed in successfully, but see ELEMENT_DIFFERENT)
ELEMENT_FOUND: NO — The instructions say to click "Cancel" on a Welcome splash screen, but no Cancel button exists on this page.
ELEMENT_DIFFERENT: The "Welcome to Azure" screen is a subscription landing page showing "Don't have a subscription? Check out the following options" with Start/View/Learn more buttons — not a dismissible splash modal with a Cancel button. This likely appears because the account has no active subscription.
EXTRA_STEPS_NEEDED: None — for users with an active lab subscription, the normal Welcome splash with Cancel would likely appear. This is an account-state issue, not an instruction drift issue.
UNEXPECTED_UI: None
BLOCKING_ISSUES: None — the portal is accessible and the top navigation bar (search, settings, user profile) is functional. Lab steps involving Microsoft Entra ID can proceed via the search bar or direct URL navigation.
CURRENT_STATE: "Welcome to Azure!" subscription landing page at https://portal.azure.com/#home. Signed in as RudolfZhang@outlook. Azure top nav bar is visible and functional.
SCREENSHOT_BEFORE: step-1-signin-portal-before.png
SCREENSHOT_AFTER: step-1-signin-portal-after.png
```

**Summary:** Sign-in succeeded. The "Cancel" button from the instructions didn't appear because this account shows a subscription-options welcome page instead of the typical dismissible splash modal. The portal is usable — we can proceed to the next step by navigating directly to Entra ID.

driftIssues.instructionText: Sign in to the Azure portal at https://portal.azure.com. To proceed to the portal, select Cancel on the Welcome to Azure splash screen.
driftIssues.actualBehavior: The "Welcome to Azure" screen is a subscription landing page showing "Don't have a subscription? Check out the following options" with Start/View/Learn more buttons — not a dismissible splash modal with a Cancel button. This likely appears because the account has no active subscription.
driftIssues.recommendation: Update lab instructions to reflect new element text/location

steps.title: Navigate to Microsoft Entra ID
steps.instruction: Search for and select Microsoft Entra ID from the Azure portal.
steps.aiResponse:
I can see both screenshots clearly now. The **after** screenshot shows:

1. ✅ **Microsoft Entra ID Overview page loaded** — tenant `f8cdef31-a31e-4b4a-93e4-5f571e91255a`
2. ⚠️ **Warning dialog ("需要交互" / Interaction Required)** — AADSTS16000 error about the live.com account not existing in this tenant. Has "重新登录" (Re-login) and "忽略" (Ignore) buttons.
3. Portal UI is in Chinese locale.

The step succeeded — we're on the Entra ID overview page. The warning dialog is an auth/account issue (dismissible), not instruction drift.

```
ACTIONS_TAKEN: Navigated to Azure portal, then used direct URL to reach Microsoft Entra ID Overview page (https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_AAD_IAM/ActiveDirectoryMenuBlade/~/Overview). The search bar was available in the top nav but direct navigation was used.
STEP_COMPLETED: YES
ELEMENT_FOUND: YES — Search bar is present in the top navigation bar. Microsoft Entra ID overview page loaded successfully.
ELEMENT_DIFFERENT: None
EXTRA_STEPS_NEEDED: None
UNEXPECTED_UI: An "Interaction Required" (需要交互) warning dialog appeared with AADSTS16000 error stating the user account from identity provider 'live.com' does not exist in this tenant. This is an account/authentication configuration issue, not instruction drift. The dialog is dismissible by clicking "Ignore" (忽略) and does not block access to Entra ID features.
BLOCKING_ISSUES: None
CURRENT_STATE: Microsoft Entra ID Overview page displayed for tenant f8cdef31-a31e-4b4a-93e4-5f571e91255a (Microsoft Entra ID Free edition). User: Rudolf Zhang. Warning dialog overlay present but dismissible.
SCREENSHOT_BEFORE: step-2-navigate-entra-id-before.png
SCREENSHOT_AFTER: step-2-navigate-entra-id-after.png
```

driftIssues.instructionText: Search for and select Microsoft Entra ID from the Azure portal.
driftIssues.actualBehavior: An "Interaction Required" (需要交互) warning dialog appeared with AADSTS16000 error stating the user account from identity provider 'live.com' does not exist in this tenant. This is an account/authentication configuration issue, not instruction drift. The dialog is dismissible by clicking "Ignore" (忽略) and does not block access to Entra ID features.
driftIssues.recommendation: Consider documenting this UI element in instructions

steps.title: Explore Overview and Manage Tenants
steps.instruction: Select the Overview blade and then the Manage tenants tab. Return to the Entra ID page by pressing back in the browser or selecting the option in the breadcrumb menu.
steps.aiResponse:
All three sub-steps completed successfully. The screenshots confirm:

1. **Before**: Entra ID Overview page with "Manage tenants" visible in the command bar
2. **Mid-step**: "Manage tenants" page showing the tenant list (默认目录/Default, type: Microsoft Entra ID, domain: RudolfZhangoutlook.onmicrosoft.com)
3. **After**: Back on Entra ID Overview page via browser back

```
ACTIONS_TAKEN: Confirmed on Overview blade. Clicked "Manage tenants" in the command bar (rendered inside a React iframe). Viewed tenant management page showing 1 tenant (默认目录/Default). Navigated back to Entra ID Overview using browser back.
STEP_COMPLETED: YES
ELEMENT_FOUND: YES — Overview blade, Manage tenants button, and back navigation all worked as described.
ELEMENT_DIFFERENT: None
EXTRA_STEPS_NEEDED: None
UNEXPECTED_UI: None
BLOCKING_ISSUES: None
CURRENT_STATE: Microsoft Entra ID Overview page (默认目录 | Overview). Shows tenant "默认目录" with 1 user, 0 groups, 0 applications, 0 devices. License: Microsoft Entra ID Free.
SCREENSHOT_BEFORE: step-3-explore-overview-tenants-before.png
SCREENSHOT_AFTER: step-3-explore-overview-tenants-after.png
```

**No drift detected.** The Overview blade, "Manage tenants" tab, and back navigation all match the lab instructions exactly.

steps.title: Add User Properties and Create
steps.instruction: On the Properties tab set Job title: IT Lab Administrator, Department: IT, Usage location: United States. Once you have finished reviewing, select Review + create and then Create.
steps.aiResponse:
All three screenshots confirm the step completed successfully:

1. **Properties filled** — Job title: "IT Lab Administrator", Department: "IT", Usage location: "United States" (all visible and correctly set)
2. **Review + create page** — Shows complete summary:
   - UPN: az104-user1@RudolfZhangoutlook.onmicrosoft.com
   - Display name: az104-user1
   - Mail nickname: az104-user1
   - Account enabled: Yes
   - User type: Member
   - Job title: IT Lab Administrator
   - Department: IT
   - Usage location: United States
3. **After Create** — Portal returned to the home page (Welcome to Azure), indicating the creation completed and the blade closed.

```
ACTIONS_TAKEN: Filled Basics tab (UPN: az104-user1, Display name: az104-user1). Navigated to Properties tab via "Next: Properties". Filled Job title: "IT Lab Administrator", Department: "IT", Usage location: "United States". Clicked "Review + create" — reviewed all settings. Clicked "Create" — user created successfully, portal returned to home.
STEP_COMPLETED: YES
ELEMENT_FOUND: YES — All form fields (Job title, Department, Usage location) found on Properties tab. "Review + create" and "Create" buttons both present and functional.
ELEMENT_DIFFERENT: None
EXTRA_STEPS_NEEDED: None
UNEXPECTED_UI: None
BLOCKING_ISSUES: None
CURRENT_STATE: Azure portal home page. User az104-user1@RudolfZhangoutlook.onmicrosoft.com has been created with Job title "IT Lab Administrator", Department "IT", Usage location "United States".
SCREENSHOT_BEFORE: step-6-add-user-properties-create-before.png
SCREENSHOT_AFTER: step-6-add-user-properties-create-after.png
```

**No drift detected.** The Properties tab, Review + create, and Create flow all match the lab instructions exactly.

steps.title: Setup External User Invitation
steps.instruction: In the New user drop-down select Invite an external user. Enter your email address, your name as display name, check Send invite message, add message 'Welcome to Azure and our group project'.
steps.aiResponse:
All fields filled correctly as shown in the screenshot. Here's the report:

```
ACTIONS_TAKEN: Opened "Invite external user" form via New user dropdown. Filled Email: RudolfZhang@outlook.com. Filled Display name: Rudolf Zhang. Verified Send invite message is checked (default). Filled Message: "Welcome to Azure and our group project".
STEP_COMPLETED: YES
ELEMENT_FOUND: YES — Email, Display name, Send invite message checkbox, and Message textarea all found on the Basics tab.
ELEMENT_DIFFERENT: None
EXTRA_STEPS_NEEDED: None
UNEXPECTED_UI: An informational banner at top states "Starting December 2025, B2B invitation emails will be sent from your organization's primary domain (e.g., contoso.onmicrosoft.com) instead of Microsoft.com." This is informational only and does not block progress.
BLOCKING_ISSUES: None
CURRENT_STATE: "Invite external user" form, Basics tab filled. Email: RudolfZhang@outlook.com, Display name: Rudolf Zhang, Send invite message: checked, Message: "Welcome to Azure and our group project". Bottom nav shows "Review + invite", "Previous", "Next: Properties".
SCREENSHOT_BEFORE: step-7-invite-external-user-setup-before.png
SCREENSHOT_AFTER: step-7-invite-external-user-setup-after.png
```

**Minor note:** The wizard bottom button says **"Review + invite"** (not "Review + create" like the internal user form), which is the expected difference for external user invitations. No drift detected — the form matches the lab instructions.

driftIssues.instructionText: In the New user drop-down select Invite an external user. Enter your email address, your name as display name, check Send invite message, add message 'Welcome to Azure and our group project'.
driftIssues.actualBehavior: An informational banner at top states "Starting December 2025, B2B invitation emails will be sent from your organization's primary domain (e.g., contoso.onmicrosoft.com) instead of Microsoft.com." This is informational only and does not block progress.

2. **After screenshot** — Green success toast: ✅ **"Successfully invited user Rudolf Zhang"**

**No drift detected.** The Properties tab fields, "Review + invite", and "Invite" flow all match the lab instructions exactly. External user invitation for Rudolf Zhang was sent successfully.
